tutorial.welcome:
スカイウォーキング: アースフライトへようこそ! 🚀
現在地から宇宙の果てまで飛び立ちましょう。
tutorial.look_around: 傾けたりパンして周囲を見回しましょう。
tutorial.altitude: 上にスワイプして高度を上げ、雲に向かって上昇しましょう。
tutorial.roll: ロールボタンで左右に傾けましょう。
tutorial.comfort: コンフォートモードを有効にして乗り物酔いを軽減しましょう。
tutorial.teleport: テレポートボタンをタップして地球上のどこにでも移動しましょう。
tutorial.screenshot: スクリーンショットボタンで素晴らしい景色を撮影しましょう。
tutorial.favorites: お気に入りの場所を保存していつでも再訪できます。

gem_son_doong_cave_desc: The world's largest cave, hidden deep in Phong Nha-Kẻ Bàng National Park in Vietnam. It contains its own weather system and jungle.
gem_son_doong_cave_fact: Son Doong is large enough to house a 40-story skyscraper inside.
gem_son_doong_light_beam_desc: A stunning beam of sunlight enters through a collapsed section of Son Doong's ceiling, illuminating the cave floor below.
gem_son_doong_light_beam_fact: The cave was first explored by a British-Vietnamese team in 2009.
gem_zhangye_danxia_desc: Multicoloured rock formations in Gansu province, China, shaped by millions of years of tectonic plate activity.
gem_zhangye_danxia_fact: The vivid colours come from layers of red sandstone and minerals deposited over 24 million years.
gem_socotra_dragon_trees_desc: Ancient dragon blood trees on Socotra Island, Yemen, with a distinctive umbrella-shaped canopy found nowhere else on Earth.
gem_socotra_dragon_trees_fact: Socotra has been called the Galápagos of the Indian Ocean for its unique biodiversity.
gem_aogashima_volcano_desc: A remote Japanese volcanic island with a volcano within a volcano, rising dramatically from the Pacific Ocean.
gem_aogashima_volcano_fact: Only about 200 people live on Aogashima, accessible only by helicopter or boat.
gem_cat_ba_lagoons_desc: Secret emerald lagoons surrounded by towering karst limestone pillars in Vietnam's Ha Long Bay.
gem_cat_ba_lagoons_fact: Many of these lagoons can only be reached by kayak through narrow sea caves.
gem_jiuzhaigou_valley_desc: A national park in Sichuan, China, famous for its multi-level waterfalls, colourful lakes, and snow-capped peaks.
gem_jiuzhaigou_valley_fact: The turquoise colour of the lakes is caused by calcium carbonate deposits on the lake beds.
gem_bagan_temples_desc: An ancient city in Myanmar containing over 2,000 Buddhist temples, pagodas, and ruins on a vast plain.
gem_bagan_temples_fact: At its peak in the 11th–13th centuries, Bagan had over 10,000 religious structures.
gem_preah_vihear_desc: A stunning Khmer cliff-top temple complex on the Dangrek Mountains, straddling the Cambodia–Thailand border.
gem_preah_vihear_fact: Preah Vihear was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2008.
gem_sigiriya_lion_rock_desc: A 5th-century rock fortress in Sri Lanka rising 200 metres from the surrounding plains, built by King Kashyapa.
gem_sigiriya_lion_rock_fact: The frescoes of the 'Cloud Maidens' on the rock face are over 1,500 years old.
gem_trolltunga_desc: A breathtaking rock formation jutting horizontally from a mountain in Norway, 700 metres above Lake Ringedalsvatnet.
gem_trolltunga_fact: Trolltunga means 'Troll's Tongue' in Norwegian. The hike to reach it takes about 10–12 hours round-trip.
gem_plitvice_underground_desc: Hidden waterfalls beneath Plitvice Lakes National Park in Croatia, part of a spectacular travertine cascade system.
gem_plitvice_underground_fact: The lakes change colour seasonally from azure to green depending on the quantity of minerals and organisms.
gem_meteora_monasteries_desc: Six Eastern Orthodox monasteries perched atop natural sandstone pillars in central Greece, dating to the 14th century.
gem_meteora_monasteries_fact: The word Meteora means 'suspended in the air' in Greek.
gem_drangarnir_arch_desc: A dramatic natural sea arch in the Faroe Islands, accessible only by boat or a challenging sea cliff traverse.
gem_drangarnir_arch_fact: Drangarnir consists of two sea stacks; the arch connects the smaller one to the cliff face.
gem_lauterbrunnen_valley_desc: A deep glacial valley in Switzerland with 72 waterfalls cascading down sheer cliffs, inspiring J.R.R. Tolkien.
gem_lauterbrunnen_valley_fact: Lauterbrunnen's Staubbach Falls plunges 300 metres — one of the highest free-falling waterfalls in Europe.
gem_skogafoss_cave_desc: A hidden cave accessible only by walking behind the thundering Skógafoss waterfall on Iceland's south coast.
gem_skogafoss_cave_fact: Legend says a Viking settler hid a chest of treasure in a cave behind Skógafoss.
gem_caminito_del_rey_desc: A narrow walkway pinned along steep rock walls of the Málaga gorge in Andalusia, Spain.
gem_caminito_del_rey_fact: Originally built in 1901 for workers maintaining the hydroelectric infrastructure.
gem_turda_salt_mine_desc: A spectacular underground salt mine in Romania converted into a tourist attraction with a Ferris wheel and rowing lake.
gem_turda_salt_mine_fact: Salt has been mined at Turda since Roman times; the mine goes 120 metres underground.
gem_externsteine_desc: Five towering sandstone pillars rising from the Teutoburg Forest in Germany, used as a sacred site since prehistoric times.
gem_externsteine_fact: The Externsteine contain a cave church carved in the 12th century with elaborate Christian reliefs.
gem_blue_grotto_capri_desc: A luminous sea cave on the coast of Capri, Italy, where sunlight passing through an underwater entrance creates an electric blue glow.
gem_blue_grotto_capri_fact: The Romans used the Blue Grotto as a private bathing spot for Emperor Tiberius.
gem_antelope_canyon_desc: A slot canyon in Arizona sculpted by flash floods, where shafts of sunlight create ethereal beams in the narrow passageway.
gem_antelope_canyon_fact: Antelope Canyon is on Navajo land and named for the pronghorn antelope that once roamed here.
gem_fly_geyser_desc: A colourful man-made geyser in Nevada, accidentally created during a well-drilling operation in 1964.
gem_fly_geyser_fact: The vivid green and red colours are caused by thermophilic algae thriving in the mineral-rich water.
gem_cenote_ik_kil_desc: A sacred Mayan cenote in Mexico's Yucatán Peninsula, a 60-metre-deep open sinkhole with hanging vines and waterfalls.
gem_cenote_ik_kil_fact: The Maya used Ik Kil for sacred ceremonies and offerings to their rain god Chaac.
gem_hamilton_pool_desc: A natural swimming hole in Texas formed when the dome of an underground river collapsed centuries ago.
gem_hamilton_pool_fact: A 15-metre waterfall feeds the jade-green pool beneath a 15-metre jade overhang.
gem_marble_canyon_bc_desc: A deep turquoise glacially-fed canyon in British Columbia, Canada, its walls streaked with vivid mineral colours.
gem_marble_canyon_bc_fact: Marble Canyon is part of Kootenay National Park and was formed by meltwater 10,000 years ago.
gem_mosquito_bay_desc: The world's most bioluminescent bay in Vieques, Puerto Rico, glowing electric blue at night from millions of dinoflagellates.
gem_mosquito_bay_fact: Mosquito Bay holds the Guinness World Record for the brightest bioluminescent bay.
gem_na_pali_caves_desc: Ancient sea caves carved into the dramatic lava cliffs of Kauai's Nā Pali Coast, accessible only by kayak or helicopter.
gem_na_pali_caves_fact: The Nā Pali cliffs rise up to 1,200 metres and are among the world's most dramatic coastlines.
gem_havasu_falls_desc: Turquoise waterfalls deep in the Grand Canyon, on the Havasupai tribal lands in Arizona.
gem_havasu_falls_fact: The travertine dams here constantly reshape the pools below the falls.
gem_mount_roraima_desc: A flat-topped tepui on the Venezuela–Brazil–Guyana border, one of the world's oldest geological formations at 1.8 billion years.
gem_mount_roraima_fact: Mount Roraima inspired Arthur Conan Doyle's novel The Lost World (1912).
gem_marble_caves_chile_desc: Swirling blue-and-white marble caverns in Chilean Patagonia, sculpted by centuries of wave action on General Carrera Lake.
gem_marble_caves_chile_fact: The colour of the marble changes with the season as lake water levels rise and fall.
gem_vinicunca_rainbow_desc: A 5,200-metre peak in the Peruvian Andes with vivid striped mineral bands of red, yellow, and green.
gem_vinicunca_rainbow_fact: Rainbow Mountain was only discovered by outsiders around 2015 when melting glaciers revealed the coloured rock.
gem_lencois_maranhenses_desc: Vast white sand dunes in northeast Brazil that fill with crystal-clear lagoons after the rainy season.
gem_lencois_maranhenses_fact: Despite appearing desert-like, Lençóis Maranhenses receives abundant rainfall and is not a true desert.
gem_perito_moreno_tunnels_desc: Glacier ice tunnels and arches carved through Perito Moreno Glacier in Argentine Patagonia as meltwater forces its way through.
gem_perito_moreno_tunnels_fact: Perito Moreno is one of very few glaciers in the world still advancing.
gem_ciudad_perdida_desc: The Lost City, an ancient tairona settlement deep in Colombia's Sierra Nevada jungle, predating Machu Picchu by 650 years.
gem_ciudad_perdida_fact: Ciudad Perdida was rediscovered in 1972 by local treasure hunters known as guaqueros.
gem_salar_de_uyuni_desc: The world's largest salt flat in Bolivia transforms into a giant mirror after rain, creating a seamless sky reflection.
gem_salar_de_uyuni_fact: Salar de Uyuni contains over 50% of the world's known lithium reserves.
gem_chapada_blue_cave_desc: A sunlight-illuminated river cave in Bahia, Brazil, where the water glows electric blue from refracted light.
gem_chapada_blue_cave_fact: The cave can only be visited during specific hours when sunlight penetrates at the correct angle.
gem_deadvlei_desc: A white clay pan in Namibia's Namib Desert filled with 900-year-old dead camelthorn trees, creating a surreal landscape.
gem_deadvlei_fact: The camel thorn trees are too dry to decompose; they have stood dead for nearly a millennium.
gem_tsingy_bemaraha_desc: A forest of razor-sharp limestone needles in western Madagascar, one of the most unique landscapes on Earth.
gem_tsingy_bemaraha_fact: Tsingy means 'where one cannot walk barefoot' in Malagasy.
gem_lake_natron_desc: A blood-red alkaline lake in Tanzania so caustic it can calcify animals, yet flamingos breed here in huge numbers.
gem_lake_natron_fact: Lake Natron's pH can reach 10.5 — caustic enough to burn the skin of unadapted animals.
gem_danakil_depression_desc: One of Earth's hottest and lowest places in Ethiopia, with vivid sulphur springs, lava lakes, and salt flats.
gem_danakil_depression_fact: Danakil sits 116 metres below sea level and regularly reaches temperatures above 50°C.
gem_blyde_river_potholes_desc: Cylindrical potholes carved by whirlpools at the confluence of two rivers in South Africa's Blyde River Canyon.
gem_blyde_river_potholes_fact: The potholes were named after a gold prospector named Tom Burke who never found the gold he sought.
gem_ennedi_arches_desc: Saharan rock arches and towers in Chad, carved by ancient rivers that have long since dried up.
gem_ennedi_arches_fact: The Ennedi Plateau contains ancient rock art depicting animals that no longer exist in the Sahara.
gem_skeleton_coast_desc: The graveyard of hundreds of ships along Namibia's treacherous fog-shrouded coast, rusting in the desert.
gem_skeleton_coast_fact: The Bushmen of the Kalahari called this coastline 'The Land God Made in Anger'.
gem_cave_of_swimmers_desc: A remote cave in Egypt's Libyan Desert with 10,000-year-old rock paintings depicting people swimming.
gem_cave_of_swimmers_fact: The cave proves the Sahara was once a fertile, water-rich landscape.
gem_waitomo_glowworm_desc: Underground caves in New Zealand's Waikato region, where thousands of glowworms (Arachnocampa luminosa) create a living starscape.
gem_waitomo_glowworm_fact: The Waitomo glowworm is unique to New Zealand and produces light through bioluminescence to attract prey.
gem_bungle_bungle_desc: Beehive-shaped sandstone towers in Western Australia's Purnululu National Park, striped with orange and black algae.
gem_bungle_bungle_fact: The Bungle Bungle Range was virtually unknown to outsiders until a film crew flew over it in 1983.
gem_milford_sound_obs_desc: A unique underwater observatory in Fiordland, New Zealand, where fresh water floating on salt water creates rare deep-sea conditions near the surface.
gem_milford_sound_obs_fact: Black coral, usually found at 40+ metres depth, grows at just 10 metres here due to the light-filtering fresh water.
gem_horizontal_falls_desc: A natural wonder in Western Australia where tidal forces push massive volumes of water through narrow gorges, creating horizontal waterfall-like flows.
gem_horizontal_falls_fact: Sir David Attenborough described Horizontal Falls as 'one of the greatest natural wonders of the world'.
gem_balls_pyramid_desc: The world's tallest sea stack, rising 562 metres from the Tasman Sea off Australia's Lord Howe Island.
gem_balls_pyramid_fact: In 2001, scientists discovered the Lord Howe Island stick insect — thought extinct for 80 years — living on Ball's Pyramid.
gem_palau_blue_holes_desc: A series of connected underwater caverns and chimneys in Palau's coral reefs, with walls covered in soft coral.
gem_palau_blue_holes_fact: The Blue Holes descend to a depth of 40 metres and are a world-renowned diving destination.
gem_champagne_pool_desc: A geothermal pool in New Zealand's Wai-O-Tapu thermal park, where carbon dioxide bubbles continuously like champagne.
gem_champagne_pool_fact: Champagne Pool is 65 metres wide and reaches temperatures of 74°C.
gem_wave_rock_desc: A natural rock formation in Western Australia shaped like a giant breaking ocean wave, 15 metres high and 110 metres long.
gem_wave_rock_fact: Wave Rock is estimated to be 2.7 billion years old — one of the oldest exposed rock surfaces on Earth.
gem_blood_falls_desc: A five-storey waterfall of red water flowing from Taylor Glacier in Antarctica, caused by iron-rich brine from a subglacial lake.
gem_blood_falls_fact: The bacteria in the subglacial lake have evolved to survive without sunlight, oxygen, or heat — giving clues to possible life on other planets.
gem_deception_island_desc: An active volcanic caldera in Antarctica where hot springs meet freezing Antarctic waters in a natural harbour.
gem_deception_island_fact: Deception Island contains the ruins of a Norwegian whaling station abandoned in 1931 after a volcanic eruption.
gem_ross_island_tunnels_desc: Ancient wind-carved ice tunnels and caves on Ross Island in Antarctica, formed at the base of Mount Erebus.
gem_ross_island_tunnels_fact: Warm volcanic gases from Mount Erebus create extensive cave systems beneath the Antarctic ice sheet.

route_share_export: ルートをエクスポート
route_nav_hud_off_path: コースアウト！ルートに戻ってください

airshow_audience_excited: 観客が盛り上がっています！
airshow_audience_ovation: スタンディングオベーション！
airshow_share_text: SWEFエアショーで{0}点を獲得しました！ ✈️
wildlife_desc_bald_eagle: 山の上昇気流を利用して舞い上がる力強い猛禽類。
wildlife_desc_seagull: 海岸や港付近によく見られる沿岸性の鳥。
wildlife_desc_albatross: 巨大な翼幅を持つ、大海原を旅する雄大な鳥。
wildlife_desc_canada_goose: 象徴的なV字編隊を形成する渡り水禽。
wildlife_desc_sparrow: 小型の雀が密集して旋回する群れ。
wildlife_desc_dolphin: 船のそばで飛び跳ねる遊び好きな海洋哺乳類。
wildlife_desc_humpback_whale: 壮大なジャンプで知られる巨大なヒゲクジラ。
wildlife_desc_blue_whale: 地球上で最も大きな動物で、上空からはめったに見られない。
wildlife_desc_salmon: 低高度から見える銀色のサーモンの群れ。
wildlife_desc_deer: 森や草原を歩き回る優雅な有蹄類。
wildlife_desc_bison: 広大な草原を横断する巨大な群れ。
wildlife_desc_butterfly: 熱帯の開けた場所を漂う色とりどりの群れ。
wildlife_desc_arctic_tern: 毎年極地の海を渡る長距離の渡り鳥。
wildlife_desc_flamingo: 浅い湿地で歩き回る鮮やかなピンクの群れ。
wildlife_desc_pelican: 密集した一列編隊で滑空する沿岸の鳥。
wildlife_collection_complete: 野生動物コレクション完成！
wildlife_bird_strike_warning: バードストライク警告
emergency_phase_checklist_active: チェックリスト実行中
emergency_radio_panpan: パンパン、パンパン、パンパン
emergency_radio_mayday: メーデー、メーデー、メーデー
emergency_radio_squawk7600: スコーク7600 — 無線故障
emergency_radio_squawk7500: スコーク7500 — ハイジャック

checklist_fuel_switch: 燃料タンクを切り替え
checklist_engine_restart: エンジン再起動を試みる
checklist_cross_feed: クロスフィードバルブを開く
checklist_divert_nearest: 最寄り空港へ代替着陸
checklist_flight_controls: フライトコントロールを確認
checklist_descend_warmer: より暖かい高度に降下
checklist_bus_tie: バスタイコンタクターを閉じる
checklist_gear_gravity: 降着装置 — 重力伸展
checklist_nav_backup: バックアップナビゲーションを選択
checklist_radio_backup: バックアップ無線を選択
checklist_autopilot_off: オートパイロットを解除
checklist_low_pass: 低空通過を要請 — タワー確認
emergency_desc_engine_failure: エンジンが1基故障しました。再起動を試み、代替着陸の準備をしてください。
emergency_desc_dual_engine_failure: 両エンジンが故障しました。直ちに最良滑空を確立しメーデーを宣言してください。
emergency_desc_fuel_starvation: 燃料供給が中断されました。タンクを切り替えて最寄り空港へ向かってください。
emergency_desc_fuel_leak: 機体から燃料が漏れています。燃料量を監視し必要に応じて代替着陸してください。
emergency_desc_bird_strike: バードストライクが検出されました。エンジンを確認し構造損傷を評価してください。
emergency_desc_structural_damage: 構造損傷が生じました。速度を下げて急激な操縦を避けてください。
emergency_desc_icing_critical: 深刻な着氷が飛行操縦面に影響しています。アンチアイスを起動して降下してください。
emergency_desc_electrical_failure: 電気系統が故障しました。電源を復旧して最寄り空港へ向かってください。
emergency_desc_hydraulic_failure: 油圧が失われました。手動操縦および着陸の準備をしてください。
emergency_desc_fire_onboard: 機内火災が検出されました。消火剤を放出してメーデーを宣言してください。
emergency_desc_depressurization: 客室与圧が失われました。直ちに酸素マスクを装着して降下してください。
emergency_desc_navigation_failure: 航法系統が故障しました。バックアップシステムを使用して管制塔に連絡してください。
emergency_desc_communication_failure: 無線通信が故障しました。スコーク7600を設定して無線途絶手順に従ってください。
emergency_desc_control_surface_jam: 操縦翼面が固着しています。差動推力を使用して制御を維持してください。
emergency_desc_landing_gear_malfunction: 降着装置が正常に展開しません。代替展開を試みてください。
training_em_dual_engine: 双発エンジン故障訓練
training_em_engine_hint: スロットルをアイドルに設定し、燃料タンクを切り替えてからエンジン再起動を試みてください。
training_em_fire_hint: 消火剤を放出し、メーデーを宣言して緊急降下を開始してください。
training_em_depress_hint: 直ちに酸素マスクを装着し、3,000メートルに降下してください。
training_em_gear_hint: 最初に重力展開を試み、次に手動展開を試みてください。タワーへの低空通過を要請してください。
training_em_fuel_hint: 満タンのタンクに切り替え、クロスフィードバルブを開いて代替着陸してください。
training_em_dual_engine_hint: 直ちに最良滑空速度を確立してメーデーを宣言してください。
checklist_throttle_idle_action: スロットルレバーをIDLEデテントに移動
checklist_fuel_switch_action: 反対側の燃料タンクを選択
checklist_engine_restart_action: エンジンスタートボタンを押してN1を監視
checklist_squawk_7700_action: トランスポンダコードを7700に設定
checklist_fuel_both_action: 燃料セレクターをBOTHに選択
checklist_apu_start_action: APUマスターとスタートボタンを押す
checklist_glide_best_action: QRHに従い最良滑空速度を確立
checklist_cross_feed_action: クロスフィードバルブを開く
checklist_fire_agent_1_action: 消火剤1ボタンを押し続ける
checklist_fire_agent_2_action: 消火剤2ボタンを押し続ける
checklist_rapid_descent_action: 最大降下率を設定し3,000m以下に降下
checklist_oxygen_masks_action: 酸素マスクを引いてシールを確認
checklist_anti_ice_on_action: すべてのアンチアイススイッチをONに選択
checklist_gear_gravity_action: 重力伸展ハンドルを引く
checklist_gear_manual_action: 手動ギアクランクを使用
checklist_low_pass_action: 視覚的ギア確認のための低空通過を要請
city_style_futuristic: フューチャリスティック
replay_theater_load_project: プロジェクトを読み込む
replay_theater_effect_pip: ピクチャーインピクチャー
academy_license_atpl: 定期運送用操縦士免許 (ATPL)
